results.title: Australian diary. No. 132
results.brief_description: Magazine items: Drought, A sheep station in N.S.W. faces ruin because of one of the worst-ever droughts; rain has not fallen for 18 months and water for limited use is brought in by truck. Bundaberg: the Queensland coastal port which serves a rich agricultural area with a near tropical climate and rich volcanic soil. Birth place of the famous airman, Bert Hinkler, it is a prosperous city which thrives on the extensive sugar cane industry. No other credits available.

results.title: Australian diary. No. 131
results.brief_description: Magazine items: Mobile classroom N.S.W. A fully equipped train with classrooms and an instructor who lives aboard travels around the rural centres of N.S.W. giving technical training to people of all ages. Football Grand Final - Melbourne. In Victoria, Australian Rules Football is the most popular winter sport. Briefly explains the game and shows the Grand Final Match between Geelong and Richmond. No other credits available.

results.title: Australian diary. No. 129
results.creator_credit: John Martin-Jones and Department of the Interior
results.brief_description: Magazine items: North West Cape; the development of the communications centre for space tracking in the north west cape area and the construction of a United States naval radio station near the new town of Exmouth. The role of the Standards Association of Australia in modern industry. The Snowy Mountains - Thredbo Alpine Village is a sign of the sudden popularity of winter sports.

results.title: Australian diary. No. 126
results.creator_credit: R.J. Edwards and Department of the Interior
results.brief_description: Magazine items: Crop protection from the air. Use of aircraft for pasture improvement and pest control has been developed to a high state of efficiency, through the combined efforts of the farmer and the flier. Guide dogs bring freedom and new life to blind people. Shows them meeting and training with their new masters at the National Guide Dog Training Centre, Kew, Vic. Coal mines close but the people of Cessnock, N.S.W. work an “industrial miracle”.
results.creator_credit_for_label: R.J. Edwards and Department of the Interior

results.title: Australian diary. No. 121
results.creator_credit: Jack S. Allan and Australian National Film Board
results.brief_description: Models give insight to work at Woomera and into the work of Australia’s part in the British Commonwealth Defence Plan; a wartime Army Tank Carrier converted into a hopper barge dredges Coralline from the bed of Moreton Bay and transports it up the Brisbane River to a cement manufacturing company; a new Cub pack forms in Canberra.
